tweets.text:
This list, from the Federal Reserve’s website, should leave no doubt that its new Chair, Kevin Warsh, is serious about reforming, transforming, and enhancing the world’s most powerful central bank.
It’s not just about the exceptional expertise, experience, and reputation of the individuals selected. It’s also about their ability to drive meaningful buy-in for improving areas that have led to recent operational and strategic slippages, thereby undermining the Federal Reserve's overall effectiveness, credibility, and political independence.
#federalreserve #centralbanks #economy #markets @federalreserve

tweets.text:
While on market levels to monitor:
The Yen has weakened to above ¥162 to the dollar (Bloomberg chart below) following a media report that local pension funds will not be pressured to purchase more domestic securities. This is adding upward pressure on yields.
Bottom line: This ambiguity is not constructive, especially for a government seeking to reverse what it views as an excessive weakening of the currency.
#economy #markets #yen #japan

tweets.text:
I have never heard edge explained so clearly in my life to be honest. 

Had the pleasure of sitting down with @gametheorizing on the @1000xPod to record some top tier content, where he articulated exactly how to identify opportunities in the markets. 

"Someone always wants to de-risk an asset they hold. They'll give up edge to secure the bag. You're always exchanging risk for expected value. Understand that primitive and you find every trade."

I think we should expect a decent flow to "sneak out" during this as was the case during the Operation Freedom and throughout May/June e.g from Abu Dhabi

Trump: "We are reinstating the THE IRANIAN BLOCKADE, so named because it is only stopping Iran’s ships or customers from entering or leaving. All other countries will have fair and open use of the Strait. The U.S.A. will be, from this point forward, known as “THE GUARDIAN OF THE HORMUZ STRAIT,” but as such, and as a matter of FAIRNESS, will be reimbursed, at the rate of 20% on all cargo shipped"

I hate high taxes, but why anyone would die on the hill defending the U.S. healthcare system is beyond my understanding. 

You can't find a system anywhere on Earth that delivers worse value for money.

Incredibly expensive. Mediocre outcome. https://t.co/TB3lZKH4AH
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
It always makes me laugh when naive US leftists talk about "free healthcare" in Denmark.

You pay a 60% top income tax rate and then 25% VAT when you want to buy anything. You can barely afford anything and then wait four months for a specialist appointment.

Nothing is free.

tweets.text:
Watching Ken Griffiths GS interview and there was some food for thought there

"Are you in the asset management business or performance business" 

Never thought of my fund this way before

There are definitely LPs who want "crypto exposure" to check that box, but I'd say those can just buy ETFs now

Imo its clear most actively managed funds should def be in the performance business

The moment you cross the lane into the world of simply bloating your AUM and charging management fees as the primary motivation for your business, you spiritually die as a professional investor

tweets.text: @hodlonaut Large OP_RETURNs don’t ”facilitate Alpen’s business model”. As Citrea already showed, their business model works perfectly fine using unspendable outputs for variable ZK proof sizes. A relaxed OP_RETURN facilitates doing what they already knew how to do without UTXO bloat.

A relaxed OP_RETURN facilitates doing what they already knew how to do without UTXO bloat.

tweets.text:
Summer sell offs don’t really need a big reason, they just need a first seller.

One ordinary red day can create an air pocket over a few sessions, and by the time everyone agrees on the fundamental explanation, index can already be 5-10% lower.

https://t.co/ss7pRCDwbU

tweets.text:
arguably the most badass team in the world right now. 

deciding where compute gets allocated in ai is the only  thing determining if anthropic maintains the lead

krishna rao (cfo) described their strategy beautifully in @InvestLikeBest interview:

> compute gets allocated to 3 places: training, serving customers and the research team.

> the biggest allocation goes to... the research team to help them design a killer new model. ai compute accelerates that process.

> i also love that anthropic's the only lab with gpu-agnostic data centers. they train claude across TPUs (google), tranium (amazon), nvidia chips etc

they train claude in the morning then use THE SAME data center to inference it in the afternoon 

so damn cool.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Personal update: I'm taking a leave of absence from YC to join Anthropic. I'll be working with @NotTomBrown on the compute team.

Powerful AI has the potential to improve the life of every human on earth and, as we enter the early stages of recursive self-improvement,
